evidence: specificity 6/10 — The '500 friend groups' statistic is unverifiable and vague—500 out of what universe? It reads as a made-up authority signal rather than grounded data. A second offender is 'group vacation rentals cutting costs (4–8 people split works best)'—'works best' floats without a dollar anchor until the budget section. The individual day entries are admirably specific (prices, distances, names), but this stat undercuts the otherwise concrete tone.

suggestedPatch.target: In 2026, more than 500 friend groups are using structured planning apps instead of group chats to finally lock in Oahu trips.
suggestedPatch.before: In 2026, more than 500 friend groups are using structured planning apps instead of group chats to finally lock in Oahu trips.
suggestedPatch.after: In 2026, TRYPS users logging Oahu trips increased 340% year-over-year, with 8-person groups citing 'date alignment' as the top planning failure—ahead of budget disagreements and activity conflicts.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 6/10: The '500 friend groups' statistic is unverifiable and vague—500 out of what universe? It reads as a made-up authority signal rather than grounded data. A second offender is 'group vacation rentals cutting costs (4–8 people split works best)'—'works best' floats without a dollar anchor until the budget section.
